# SC-14: API Security Testing

**Track:** Security Controls (`SC`) · **Priority:** MUST (mandatory) · **Status:** draft

Execute dedicated API security testing against all exposed API endpoints using OWASP API Security Top 10 methodology. Test for: Broken Object Level Authorisation (BOLA), Broken Function Level Authorisation (BFLA), Broken Authentication, Unrestricted Resource Consumption, Broken Object Property Level Authorisation, Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF), Security Misconfiguration, Improper Inventory Management, API rate limiting, and unsafe consumption of downstream APIs. Complement SC-13 (DAST) which tests general OWASP Top 10.

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 4 | Testing & Documentation | Mandatory | *API security testing is distinct from general DAST (SC-13) and must target API-specific attack classes. Includes REST, GraphQL, WebSocket, and RPC endpoints.*

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Agent executes, human reviews findings |
| **Agent does** | Execute OWASP API Security Top 10 testing using automated tools (Burp Suite, OWASP ZAP API scanner, or equivalent). Perform fuzzing, schema validation testing, authentication boundary testing, and authorisation logic testing. Generate findings report with severity ratings and remediation guidance. |
| **Human does** | Review findings. Determine which vulnerabilities must be remediated before deployment (blocker), which can be accepted as known risks (exception), and which are false positives. Approve remediation or exception for each finding. |

## Inputs

| Artifact | Source Control | Description |
|----------|---------------|-------------|
| API specification (OpenAPI/Swagger, GraphQL schema, etc.) | Definition of all exposed API endpoints, operations, and parameters |  |
| Deployed system under test | Staging/test environment with deployed APIs ready for testing |  |
| Test data | Valid and invalid test payloads for fuzzing and boundary testing |  |


## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| API Security Test Report | report | Findings by OWASP API Top 10 category, including affected endpoints, attack vectors, severity ratings (CVSS), and remediation guidance | 7 years (regulatory audit trail) |
| Remediation tracking | decision | For each finding: remediation status, owner, target fix date, and approval sign-off | 7 years |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | per_release |
| **SLA** | 120 min |
| **Tooling** | Burp Suite Professional (API scanner module), OWASP ZAP (API fuzzing plugin), Postman Security, 42Crunch API Security |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** | escalate |
| **Escalate to** | Security Team Lead |

Critical API vulnerabilities (BOLA exposing sensitive data, BFLA allowing privilege escalation, SSRF enabling lateral movement) must be remediated before deployment. If remediation cannot be completed, escalate to security leadership for risk decision.

## Dependencies

- `SC-12`
- `SC-13`
- `QC-06`


## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| DORA | Art. 24-25 | Comprehensive digital operational resilience testing must include security assessments and gap analyses of exposed interfaces |
| DORA | Art. 9(2) | ICT systems must be protected from unauthorised access; API boundaries are primary attack surface |
| EU AI Act | Art. 15(3-5) | AI systems must be resilient against attempts to alter use, outputs or performance. API endpoints are primary vectors for adversarial attacks on AI systems. |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** Security · **Owner role:** Application Security Engineer
